perturbation.original_text: 10.5 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of New York applicable to contracts made and to be performed exclusively in that State without giving effect to the principles of conflict of laws.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada applicable to contracts made and to be performed exclusively in that State without giving effect to the principles of conflict of laws.
perturbation.explanation: The governing law has been changed from New York to Nevada in the 'Site; Site Management' Section. Since the New Jersey corporation is located in New Jersey, and the contract mentions that the site is for consumers on the Internet, this should have been placed in the General Provisions section so that it is more easily located.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General principles of contract law and jurisdictional rules; also New York Choice of Law rules.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY CLS Gen Oblig § 5-1401 outlines conditions under which New York law can govern contracts, particularly when the agreement involves a certain monetary threshold and a connection to the state. Moving the choice of law provision to an earlier, less prominent section and changing it to Nevada law, may obscure the parties' intentions regarding which state's laws should apply, potentially leading to disputes over enforceability, especially since one of the companies is located in New Jersey.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: NYS Open Legislation | NYSenate.gov Sorry, you need to enable JavaScript to visit this website. Skip to main content The requested entry could not be found. Try searching OpenLegislation Browse all Laws of New York
perturbation.justification: The change in governing law from New York to Nevada, coupled with its relocation to a potentially less noticeable section, introduces a structural flaw that could create a legal contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation notes the importance of placement for easy location, and the Law Explanation suggests this change could obscure the parties' intentions. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is unavailable, the rationale provided highlights how the altered structure and choice of law could lead to disputes over enforceability, especially considering the location of the involved company in New Jersey. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction, where the placement of a required legal disclosure in an irrelevant section makes it difficult to interpret, potentially undermining the agreement's enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 Notices. Any notice under this Agreement will be in writing and delivered by personal delivery, express courier, confirmed facsimile, or certified or registered mail, return receipt requested and will be deemed given upon personal delivery, one (1) day after deposit with express courier, upon confirmation of receipt of facsimile or five (5),days after deposit in the mail. Notices will be sent to a Party at its address set forth above or such other address as that Party may specify in writing pursuant to this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Notices. Any notice under this Agreement will be in writing and delivered by personal delivery, or certified or registered mail, return receipt requested and will be deemed given upon personal delivery, or five (5) days after deposit in the mail. Notices will be sent to a Party at its address set forth above or such other address as that Party may specify in writing pursuant to this Section.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Notices' section has been moved from 'General Provisions' to 'Company's Responsibilities,' omitting the options for express courier and confirmed facsimile. Removing these options may not be legally compliant with best practices for ensuring adequate notice, especially when urgent communication is needed. Also it is now within a section that is not for General Provisions, reducing readability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General contract law principles regarding adequate notice and opportunity to respond.
perturbation.law_explanation: General contract law principles dictate that notice provisions must be reasonable and afford a fair opportunity for the recipient to respond. Relocating the notice provision to a less conspicuous section and removing methods like express courier and facsimile, which offer faster confirmation, might be deemed unreasonable, particularly if the agreement requires timely action from either party. This may lead to disputes over whether proper notice was given, impacting contract enforcement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: notice | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you notice Notice is the constitutional requirement that a party being brought into court be informed of the controversy . Due Process clauses of the United States Constitution prohibit courts from hearing a case that could adversely affect a party's interest unless that party has been given proper notice. To satisfy this notice requirement, notice must be reasonably calculated, under the circumstances, to inform all interested parties that a lawsuit is pending and that it could adversely affect their interests. The degree of required notice varies depending on what type of jurisdiction a court intends to exercise. To exercise in personam jurisdiction, in-hand service of process is usually required. For in rem actions, which can affect the interests of anyone in the world, the plaintiff usually must inform all known parties of interest by a reasonably reliable means, and may then inform the rest of the world by "notice by publication" - purchasing a notice in a local newspaper multiple times over a period of several weeks. In tricky cases, courts work with plaintiffs to identify the best way of meeting notice requirements. In some unusual circumstances, a court may find traditional methods of notice are not satisfactory for a particular party. Notice is not a mere formality, as the Supreme Court recently discussed in Jones v. Flowers . In Jones v. Flowers , a state seized and sold property for unpaid property taxes. A certified letter meant to notify the owner of the impending sale was returned to the state as "unclaimed." Afterwards, the state made no additional attempt to contact the owner directly. The Court rejected the state's notice as insufficient, emphasizing that notice “must be such as one desirous of actually informing the absentee might reasonably adopt to accomplish it." Because of the constitutional importance of ensuring proper notice, courts will not forgive notice achieved through improper means even where a party actually learns of the controversy. [Last reviewed in October of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex CIVICS the Constitution THE LEGAL PROCESS wex articles civil procedure constitutional law courts and procedure individual rights Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modification of the contract's notice provision creates a contradiction by removing express courier and confirmed facsimile as acceptable methods of notice and relocating it within the contract's structure. The scraped law snippet emphasizes that notice must be 'reasonably calculated, under the circumstances, to inform all interested parties.' Removing methods like express courier and facsimile, which offer faster confirmation, may not satisfy this requirement, especially if the agreement requires timely action. The placement of the notice provision within 'Company's Responsibilities' rather than a general provision further obscures its importance, potentially leading to disputes over proper notice.

perturbation.original_text: 10.7 Entire Agreement. This Agreement is the complete and exclusive agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, superseding any prior agreements and communications (both written and oral) regarding such subject matter. This Agreement may only be modified, or any rights under it waived, by a written document executed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.3 Entire Agreement. This Agreement is the complete and exclusive agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may only be modified by a written document executed by one Party.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Entire Agreement' clause is moved to the 'Term; Termination; Termination Payment' section, and a critical part of the clause is modified. The clause now implies that the agreement can be modified by only ONE party, which is contradictory to contract law. A contract usually requires consent between parties to modify it.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Basic principles of contract law regarding mutual agreement for modifications.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-209 outlines the requirements for contract modification under the Uniform Commercial Code, emphasizing the need for agreement between the parties. By relocating the 'Entire Agreement' clause and altering it to allow modification by a single party, the contract contradicts fundamental principles of contract law. This modification would likely render the modification clause unenforceable and could lead to legal challenges regarding the validity of any unilateral changes made to the agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-209. Modification, Rescission and Waiver. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-209. Modification, Rescission and Waiver. (1) An agreement modifying a contract within this Article needs no consideration to be binding. (2) A signed agreement which excludes modification or rescission except by a signed writing cannot be otherwise modified or rescinded, but except as between merchants such a requirement on a form supplied by the merchant must be separately signed by the other party. (3) The requirements of the statute of frauds section of this Article (Section 2-201 ) must be satisfied if the contract as modified is within its provisions. (4)Although an attempt at modification or rescission does not satisfy the requirements of subsection (2) or (3) it can operate as a waiver. (5) A party who has made a waiver affecting an executory portion of the contract may retract the waiver by reasonable notification received by the other party that strict performance will be required of any term waived, unless the retraction would be unjust in view of a material change of position in reliance on the waiver. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text states that the agreement can be modified by only ONE party, whereas contract law requires agreement between BOTH parties to modify a contract, as described in the Perturbation Explanation. UCC § 2-209(1), provided in Scraped Law Snippet 1, implies the need for agreement between parties by stating that 'An agreement modifying a contract within this Article needs no consideration to be binding,' indicating mutual agreement. By allowing modification by only one party, the changed text contradicts this fundamental principle of contract modification.
